title: About the Post-enumeration Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Post-enumeration Survey
21 September 2023, 10:45am
The Post-enumeration Survey (PES) is a survey of a sample of households conducted to measure the completeness of the census count. The survey is carried out every five years after the Census of Population and Dwellings.
The nationwide survey identifies how many people were missed in the recent census, counted more than once, or counted in error. The results of PES enable New Zealand's population data collected from the census to be accurately updated.

## 2023 Post-enumeration Survey (PES)
For the 2023 Post-enumeration Survey, there are two stages and two staggered collection periods.
The first stage, undertaken before the census, known as Enumeration – involved visual checks on houses, apartment blocks and dwellings, and took place in October and November 2022 to check the representative sample of dwellings.
The second stage – Interview – the short doorstep interview – is being conducted throughout June to mid-August 2023 across all areas of New Zealand apart from the Auckland region. The PES Interviews for the Auckland region commence in July 2023 until mid-September 2023.

## Taking part in the Post-enumeration Survey
If your household is selected to take part in the Post-enumeration Survey, a local Stats NZ interviewer will visit to conduct a short interview face-to-face. There are no forms to complete – the interviewer will fill in the survey questionnaire with your answers using a tablet.
Approximately 16,500 households have been selected for an interview. Stats NZ will interview all residents within selected households, as well as any visitors. For those aged under 15 years, the interviewer will ask a responsible adult to answer on their behalf.

## Why was our household selected?
Stats NZ uses a fair statistical method based on physical addresses to select households for interviews. These chosen households make up a sample that represents a group of people.

## How long does it take?
The interview generally takes less than 10 minutes for each person to complete.

## Does our household have to take part in the Post-enumeration Survey?
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Post-enumeration Survey.

## Is the survey confidential?
The information you provide for this survey will be kept private, secure, and confidential by Stats NZ, and it will only be used by Stats NZ and trusted researchers to produce statistics and research that benefit New Zealand. There's more on how Stats NZ keeps your data safe at Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data

## Thank you for participating in the Post-enumeration Survey
The survey is vital to ensure accurate population statistics are produced from the census. These statistics are used for planning community services like schools, health care and hospitals, roading, and recreational facilities.
For more information or if you have any questions about the Post-enumeration Survey, email pes@stats.govt.nz
